steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage establishes facts about the Federalist Papers (what they are, who wrote them, when and where published, why they mattered), then pivots to something not completely known: the authorship of certain essays. The correct choice must reflect this structure — established facts followed by a specific authorship uncertainty.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The text lists the authors of a collection of essays and then notes that some of the essays were written by one person, while others were written by two people.: does not accurately reflect the uncertainty described in the passage. ✗
  • Choice B — The text describes why a collection of essays is notable and then details its publication history.: omits the authorship uncertainty that is central to the passage's pivot. ✗
  • Choice C — The text summarizes an argument made in a collection of essays and then suggests that the essays' authors didn't unanimously agree with the argument.: the passage does not describe the authors disagreeing with each other. ✗
  • Choice D — The text mentions a collection of essays and then points out something about these essays that is not completely known.: accurately captures both the established facts and the specific unresolved authorship question. ✓
D is correct.
